approvalChecklist.evidence: Research report recommendation supports lean validation.
Product definition uses a starter kit and clear purpose.
Phase 1 keeps the product simple and low-tooling.
Variant matrix prioritizes compact starter packaging.
Roadmap includes standard, premium, bundle, and expansion paths.
Product story and differentiator are explicit.
Variants include accessories, subscription, and expansion packs.

constraintsReport.manufacturingCapability: Start with simple, low-tooling production until demand is proven.
constraintsReport.moq: Prefer low MOQ or manual pilot batch.
constraintsReport.safety: Validate category-specific safety and labeling before production.
constraintsReport.shipping: Keep the starter variant lightweight and compact.
constraintsReport.targetMargin: Target 55-65 percent gross margin before paid ads.

knowledgeBaseEntries.futureImprovement: Re-score after supplier, BOM, and packaging data are available.
knowledgeBaseEntries.lesson: Start with a compact product family seed instead of a single-product dead end.

knowledgeBaseEntries.futureImprovement: Revisit alternatives if manufacturing or supplier constraints change.
knowledgeBaseEntries.lesson: Weak alternatives should be retained with reasons so future product decisions improve.

productArchitecture.versioningModel: Starter validates core architecture; Standard and Premium expand contents and packaging.

assumptions: Sprint 3 Phase 1 uses deterministic product intelligence until live supplier data is connected.
Manufacturing, BOM, and supplier validation happen in later Sprint 3 phases.
Founder should validate demand before inventory purchase.
productDefinitionDocument.differentiator: A focused starter product with planned variants, lightweight shipping, and a brand story tied to the research insight.

evidence.snippet: The idea 'Build a premium educational wooden toy business for children aged 3-5 in India.' fits a giftable, passion-led niche where content can validate demand before inventory scale.
evidence.title: EMP-001 deterministic research assumption for Build a premium educational wooden toy business for children aged 3-5 in India.

evidence.snippet: Competitors for 'Build a premium educational wooden toy business for children aged 3-5 in India.' are likely split between generic merchandise sellers and premium coffee accessory brands.
evidence.title: EMP-002 deterministic research assumption for Build a premium educational wooden toy business for children aged 3-5 in India.

evidence.snippet: Likely customers for 'Build a premium educational wooden toy business for children aged 3-5 in India.' are urban coffee drinkers, gifting buyers, and office/home café enthusiasts.
evidence.title: EMP-003 deterministic research assumption for Build a premium educational wooden toy business for children aged 3-5 in India.

evidence.snippet: The first product set for 'Build a premium educational wooden toy business for children aged 3-5 in India.' should be lightweight, visual, easy to ship, and bundle-friendly.

productDefinitionDocument.problemSolved: Gives the target customer a clear, giftable, identity-led product instead of generic marketplace merchandise.
productDefinitionDocument.productStory: Luma Logic Cubes turns the validated research opportunity into a simple product family that can be tested before inventory scale.
productDefinitionDocument.purpose: Help children aged 3-5 build logic, motor skills, and pattern recognition through a premium wooden play experience.
productDefinitionDocument.recommendedMaterial: Lightweight printable or packable materials selected for low-risk MVP validation.

targetCustomer.primarySegment: Parents of children aged 3-5 in India

productDefinitionDocument.useCase: Starter purchase, gifting, validation launch, and later product-family expansion.

productStrategy.pricingPhilosophy: Protect gross margin first, then use bundles and premium variants to lift average order value.
